incident_report:
# Incident Report: Dungeon Run Seed 30

Agent A encountered persistent pathfinding failures throughout the run, triggering spurious_block anomalies on turns 2, 9, 17, 18, and 32 despite no actual obstacles being present. Agent B operated more successfully, reaching the key pickup on turn 37, but the run ultimately failed due to exceeding the 50-turn limit (finishing at turn 51) without unlocking the target door. The critical decision point occurred around turn 37 when Agent B obtained the key, but subsequent movement (turn 41) was again blocked by a spurious_block anomaly, preventing door unlock. The root cause appears to be a combination of pathfinding instability in Agent A and insufficient time allocation—the agents needed at least one additional turn to utilize the key and reach the exit, but systematic movement anomalies consumed available turns before this could occur.
